title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police alert public to fake parking violation notices appearing to be from Boston Municipal Court.
Scam notices include fake judge names, case numbers, and QR codes for unauthorized payments.
Residents are urged to verify parking tickets through the city's website or report scams to CrimeStoppers.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, of Mansfield, was arrested for unlawfully possessing ammunition as a convicted felon, say federal prosecutors.
Authorities found firearm parts, a 5.7-caliber pistol, and ammunition in Kennedy's home despite his prior felony convictions.
Kennedy faces up to 15 years in prison and a $250,000 fine if convicted on the current ammunition possession charge.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward impresses with 10 hits and leads the Red Sox with 12 stolen bases in spring training.
Max Ferguson showcases versatility and leads the Red Sox in RBIs with 7, maintaining a .821 OPS in 17 at bats.
Tayron Guerrero delivers solid relief performance with 6 strikeouts and 1 earned run in 6 innings during spring training.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson remains with the Bruins despite trade interest, ranking second in 5-on-5 goals this season.
Coach Marco Sturm supports retaining Arvidsson for his scoring ability, leadership, and potential ongoing role.
Boston may consider extending Arvidsson's contract to stabilize their forward line amid upcoming roster changes.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are seeking a new left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Jared Wilson is expected to move from left guard to center, his college position, for the Patriots.
Potential replacements for the Patriots include Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, Elgton Jenkins, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, from Stockton, California, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish children.
Perry has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, inspired by the resilience of children facing medical challenges.
The Boston Marathon's 'Why I’m Running' series highlights Perry's mission to bring hope to wish children and their families.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots expected to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe in free agency, per ESPN's Dan Graziano.
Mafe's projected contract reportedly set at three years, $48 million with $25 million guaranteed.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts receiver Alec Pierce, who may join Chargers on three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at the House of Blues in Boston on March 6, 2026, showcasing his political protest songs.
The concert included 13 original songs spanning Welles' 14 albums, alongside covers of popular tracks by Black Sabbath and Radiohead.
Welles' performance mixed country and alt-rock influences, utilizing sharp sarcasm in songs critiquing war, authority, and societal issues.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a 387-foot home run in Japan's 8-6 World Baseball Classic win over Korea.
Yoshida went 2-for-4 with three RBIs, helping Japan secure a 2-0 record in pool play.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at Tokyo Dome, alongside his wife, Ann Michael Maye.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, from Roslindale, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is participating with Team Eye and Ear to support Mass. Eye and Ear, where she's been a patient for 10 years.
Aboujaoude-Quirk, also a Boston Public Schools teacher, aims to raise funds for the hospital's research and emergency services.
